title: Introduction
description: NOC 63202 – Bakers | GTR Immigration Bakers under NOC 63202 are the skilled artisans and production experts essential to Canada’s retail, wholesale, and hospitality sectors. Beyond crafting breads and pastries, these TEER 3 professionals manage production schedules, ensure quality control, and maintain the high standards of Canada’s food industry. For immigration, this role is a powerful vehicle for Permanent Residency (PR), as it is frequently targeted in Category-Based Selection draws (specifically for Trades) and specialized Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level3Major Group63 – Occupations in sales and servicesSub-Major Group632 – Occupations in servicesMinor Group6320 – Cooks, butchers and bakersNOC Code63202

duties: Prepare dough for pies, bread, rolls and sweet goods, and batters for muffins, cookies and cakes according to recipes or special orders.
Bake mixed doughs and batters in industrial or retail ovens.
Frost and decorate cakes or other baked goods with precision.
Ensure quality of products meets established health and safety standards.
Draw up production schedules to determine type and quantity of goods to produce.
Purchase baking supplies and maintain inventory levels.
May hire, train, and supervise baking personnel and kitchen staff.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a three- or four-year apprenticeship program for bakers OR completion of a college program for bakers is usually required.
Several years of commercial baking experience may be accepted in lieu of formal education by some employers.
Red Seal Endorsement is available to qualified bakers and is highly regarded for interprovincial mobility.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Over the next decade, retirements will be the primary driver of job openings. The industry is also evolving with "Smart Bakery" technology, increasing the need for supervisors and technically proficient bakers who can manage automated equipment.
labour_market_demand: The demand for bakers remains consistent across Canada, with a Balanced to Moderate outlook. As consumer preferences shift toward artisanal, organic, and specialty baked goods, there is a specific shortage of bakers with technical expertise in recipe development and high-volume production. Major employers include supermarket chains (e.g., Loblaws, Sobeys), commercial bakeries, and high-end hotels in urban centers like Toronto, Montreal, and Calgary.
Trade Recognition: Access to the Red Seal Endorsement, which allows for interprovincial mobility and higher pay.
Entrepreneurial Opportunities: Many bakers in Canada eventually transition into owning their own successful boutique bakeries or cafes.
Stable Industry: Employment is spread across retail (43%), manufacturing (33%), and accommodation/food services (23%), providing diverse job security.
PR Advantage: Frequent inclusion in "In-Demand" lists for rural and Atlantic immigration programs.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted invitations for candidates with experience in "Trades" occupations.
Ontario Express Entry (Skilled Trades Stream): Specifically mentions Bakers (Minor Group 633/6332) as an eligible occupation.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For bakers with a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Canadian Experience Class (CEC): The fastest route for those already working in a Canadian bakery for 12 months.
